_meta.description: Hardware and grid configuration for THIS study's CO2 calculations
_meta.formula: CO2e(kg) = runtime_hours × n_GPU × TDP_watts × PUE × CI_gCO2_per_kWh × 1e-6
_meta.formula_source: Green Algorithms framework (Lannelongue et al., Advanced Science, 2021)

gpu.name: NVIDIA A100 80GB PCIe
gpu.tdp_note: A100 80GB PCIe TDP = 300W per NVIDIA datasheet (the SXM variant is 400W; this study used PCIe)

pretraining_gpu.name: NVIDIA H200 NVL
pretraining_gpu.tdp_note: H200 NVL TDP = 600W per NVIDIA datasheet (the SXM variant is 700W; this study used NVL)

training.epochs_note: All six benchmark models trained for 1000 epochs on a single A100 80GB PCIe GPU

self_supervised_pretraining.gpu_hours_note: Self-supervised pretraining required ~50 GPU-hours on a single NVIDIA H200 NVL GPU (one-time cost, shared across the study)

facility.pue_note: Set to 1.1 to be comparable with other foundation-model studies (e.g., DINOv2, Oquab et al. 2024); within the typical European university HPC range of 1.1-1.5
facility.ci_note: Switzerland 2023 grid carbon intensity = 41 gCO2/kWh (Ember, Yearly Electricity Data 2024 release, 2023 values). Production/territorial basis; consumption-based (incl. imports) is higher.

grid_scenarios_gCO2_per_kWh._note: Four cited grid carbon intensities used in Supplementary Figure S2, single consistent source.
grid_scenarios_gCO2_per_kWh.source: Ember, Global Electricity Review 2024 and European Electricity Review 2024 (2023 values); mirrored by Our World in Data, 'Carbon intensity of electricity'

assumptions.energy_model: GPU TDP-based; CPU and RAM energy not included
assumptions.scope: Scope 2 operational GPU emissions only (no CPU/RAM term, no embodied carbon)
assumptions.note: Energy estimated as runtime × GPU_TDP × PUE. This approximates GPU operational energy; it omits the CPU/RAM term of the full Green Algorithms model and embodied hardware emissions.

reference: Lannelongue L, Grealey J, Inouye M. Green Algorithms: Quantifying the Carbon Footprint of Computation. Advanced Science. 2021;8(12):2100707.

reference_comparison.activity: Driving an average European passenger car
reference_comparison.car_factor_source: Green Algorithms (Lannelongue et al., Advanced Science 2021): ~175 gCO2e/km for an average European passenger car (same comparator used by Selvan et al. 2022)
